data.title: Euro zone government bond yields edge up before economic data
data.description:
By Stefano Rebaudo

Euro zone government bond yields slightly extended their previous day's rise on Friday, as investors remained cautious ahead of key economic data from both the euro area and the U.S. later in the session.

Purchasing Managers’ Index readings from France, Germany and the wider euro area are due Friday morning, while the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics is set to release its inflation report later in the session.

Germany’s 10-year Bund yields were up 0.5 basis points (bps) at 2.58%.

Borrowing costs on both sides of the Atlantic rose on Thursday after U.S. sanctions on Russia prompted a jump in oil prices stoking inflation concerns.

Benchmark U.S. Treaury 10-year yields rose one bp to 4.0% in early London trade, after rising 3.5 bps the day before.

Money markets priced in a 60% chance of a 25-basis-point ECB rate cut by July (EURESTECBM7X8=ICAP). The key rate is seen at 1.84% in December 2026 (EURESTECBM10X11=ICAP) from the current 2%.

Germany’s 2-year yields (DE2YT=RR), more sensitive to expectations for ECB policy rate outlook, rose 0.5 bps to 1.94%.

The yield gap between safe-haven Bunds and 10-year French government bonds (DE10FR10=RR) — a market gauge of the risk premium investors demand to hold French debt — was at 79.50.

It hit 87.96 bps earlier this month, the highest level since January 13, on concerns about the French fiscal outlook but fell to below 75 bps after Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu survived two no-confidence votes in parliament last week.

(06:33 GMT) NatWest Lifts Guidance Again After Strong Customer Activity Boosts Quarter

By Elena Vardon

NatWest Group raised its revenue and profitability guidance for the second time this year after strong customer activity helped its third-quarter results top market expectations.

The U.K. high-street bank said operating pretax profit jumped 30% on year to 2.18 billion pounds ($2.91 billion) for the three months ended Sept. 30. Analysts had expected 1.83 billion pounds, according to a company-compiled consensus.

The lender reported a 16% increase in total income to 4.33 billion pounds, beating a 4.10 billion-pound forecast.

NatWest, which is known for issuing conservative financial targets, said it now expects total income excluding notable items for 2025 to come in around 16.3 billion pounds as loan growth and structural hedges continue to mitigate the impact of interest-rate cuts by the Bank of England. This compares with its previous guidance of income greater than 16.0 billion pounds, and current analysts' expectations of 16.22 billion pounds.

The London-listed group also raised its annual return on tangible equity forecast--a key measure of profitability--to more than 18.0%, after reporting a better-than-expected third-quarter return of 22.3%. Previous guidance was for a return greater than 16.5%, while consensus pencils in 17.3% for the year.

Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com

data.title: Kering's Turnaround Requires a Meaningful Improvement in China — Market Talk
data.description: Kering's recovery needs better prospects in the key Chinese market, Berenberg analysts write in a research note. "Under new CEO Luca de Meo, Kering is rapidly becoming a better business," the analysts say. The outlook for the French luxury group, however, is highly geared toward both the Chinese and less wealthy consumer segments, to which it is highly exposed, Berenberg says. While improving, trends in China remain weak, they say. Furthermore, the response to the new Gucci offer was described by the group as pretty good, but not at the same level as seen in the U.S., the analysts add. Berenberg reiterates its sell rating on the stock. Shares closed at 344.95 euros on Thursday. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

data.title: Eni Q3 Proforma Adj. EBIT Declines
data.description:
ROME (dpa-AFX) - Eni reported third quarter net profit attributable to shareholders of 803 million euros compared to 522 million euros, prior year. Earnings per share was 0.24 euros compared to 0.16 euros. Proforma adjusted EBIT was 3 billion euros, 12% lower than the year-ago quarter. Adjusted net profit attributable to shareholders was 1.25 billion euros compared to 1.27 billion euros, last year.

Sales from operations declined to 20.20 billion euros from 20.66 billion euros, a year ago. Total revenues were 20.55 billion euros compared to 21.02 billion euros. Oil & gas production rose 6% year-over-year to 1.76 mln boe/d.

Eni said it is raising 2025 share buy-back commitment by 0.3 billion euros to 1.8 billion euros. The Group increased expected CFFO before working capital adjustments to 12 billion euros from the previous 11.5 billion euros. Eni raised expected oil and gas production guidance for 2025 to a 1.71-1.72 mln boe/d range.

For more earnings news, earnings calendar, and earnings for stocks, visit rttnews.com.
